http.hero.title: AI Box HTTP Event Push API
http.hero.subtitle: Applicable when the platform has configured a third-party HTTP URL; the device pushes algorithm events to the third-party system.
http.basic.requestUrl.desc: URL configured by the user on the platform page
http.basic.successCriteria.desc: HTTP status code is 200, and the response body should be valid JSON, e.g. {"resCode":1}.
http.basic.note: During HTTP push, image fields are converted to base64 strings; video fields contain local file paths on the device. The video file may still be generating when the event is produced — third-party systems should wait a few seconds before retrieving the video after receiving the event.
http.field.messageId.desc: Unique event ID for tracing and retry deduplication.
http.field.algorithmCode.desc: Algorithm code. In the current implementation, this is usually the same as algorithmId.
http.field.areaId.desc: Region ID where the event occurred.
http.field.areaName.desc: Region name where the event occurred.
http.field.orignalPicture.desc: Original full-scene snapshot. The field name is historically spelled as orignalPicture for backward compatibility.
http.field.fullPicture.desc: Full-scene snapshot with detection bounding boxes overlaid.
http.field.detectedPicture.desc: Cropped detection target image; may be empty.
http.field.video.desc: Alarm video file path; may be empty.
http.field.videostructured.desc: File path for structured data (targets and regions) corresponding to the video; may be empty.
http.field.overviewFile.desc: Event process overview file path; may be empty.
http.field.recordId.desc: Target or alarm record ID. When tracking is enabled, this usually remains the same for the same target.
http.field.isRetryMessage.desc: Whether this is an offline retry message.
http.field.property.desc: Algorithm extended attributes. Only present when the corresponding algorithm has extended attributes.
http.propertyRules.desc: property outputs the corresponding sub-object based on algorithm type. Not all sub-objects appear simultaneously.
http.sub.peopleCounting: People Count, Vehicle Flow, Single-Frame Count, and Crowd Gathering
